latest.title: Connect Any MCP Client to Your Bedrock AgentCore Runtime hosted MCP Server
latest.description: MCP servers hosted on Bedrock AgentCore Runtime require AWS IAM SigV4 signing, but MCP clients like Kiro IDE, Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Cline don't support it natively. This tutorial walks through setting up mcp-proxy-for-aws — a lightweight local bridge that handles SigV4 automatically. Covers three install options (pip, uvx, Docker), client-specific JSON configs and proxy tuning parameters.

latest.title: 开源 1M+ RLHF 数据集：构建基于双子星协议 (Double Star) 的大模型存在主义安全对齐架构
latest.description: 本文介绍了一种全新的 LLM 认知架构（13-Sephirot AI），通过双轨机制（理智与慈爱）和 100万条开源对齐语料，解决大模型生成虚无主义和伤害引导的存在主义对齐难题。附 Hugging Face 开源地址与架构白皮书。

latest.description: Your New Home for Cloud Innovation

latest.title: How I Passed the AWS AI Practitioner Exam in 10 Days Using AWS Skill Builder
latest.description:
Last summer, I booked the AWS AI Practitioner exam and gave myself 10 days to learn everything from scratch - zero AI background, just a goal and a deadline. Internship season was coming up and I wanted something concrete on my resume.
Here's exactly how I did it.

latest.title: From Learning to Building: My First AWS Project as a Student
latest.description: Turning cloud basics into a real project using AWS (step-by-step for beginners)

latest.title: Building Is What Makes It Real
latest.description: At Cal Poly Pomona, I have built my foundation through coursework in data structures, algorithms, system programming, computer architecture, and object-oriented programming. But the moments that shaped me most came when I started applying what I learned outside the classroom.

latest.title: AWS Tools to Know Before Your First Hackathon
latest.description: Before you sprint into your first hackathon, get hands-on with the three AWS services that can take your build from idea to working demo — fast. Based on real experience at the Colorado State University Hackathon.

latest.title: Your Degree Is The Gym - AWS Builder Center Is The Weights
latest.description: IT DevOps student and AWS Student Builder Campus Leader sharing how AWS Builder Center bridges the gap between a college degree and a real cloud career, one click at a time.

latest.title: Automate Reviews in GitHub Actions with Kiro Headless Mode
latest.description: A step-by-step guide to adding AI-powered infrastructure review to your CI/CD pipeline using Kiro CLI with read-only permissions. Includes a working GitHub Actions workflow and real results from an NVIDIA NIM on EKS deployment.

latest.title: Launching AWS Cloud Club at BSIOTR: From Exploration to Leadership 🚀
latest.description:
The journey into cloud computing often starts with curiosity—but sometimes, it quickly turns into something much bigger.

I’m excited to share that we have officially inaugurated the AWS Cloud Club at BSIOTR, marking the beginning of a new tech-driven community focused on learning, building, and growing together.

As part of the global AWS Cloud Clubs initiative, our goal is to empower students with cloud skills and real-world exposure to modern technologies.

latest.title: La transportación cuántica y la radiación ionizante
latest.description: Transportar, crear, ionizar, descomponer, componer, integrar, calcular.

latest.title: When CoreDNS Becomes the Bottleneck: A Stress-Testing Story on EKS with kube-burner
latest.description: A heavy upload/download workload on EKS exposed CoreDNS as the real bottleneck. This post shows how kube-burner helped stress-test DNS and plan capacity.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: CarbonZero
latest.description: AI-Powered Carbon Accounting & Gamified Compliance for African Businesses and Individuals. Built with Kiro and AWS, CarbonZero helps African SMEs and exporters track emissions, achieve EU CBAM compliance, and turn sustainability into a competitive advantage — now with the audit trails, carbon passports, and accounting sync to make it real.

latest.title: The AWS Cloud Infrastructure: A Comprehensive Technical Analysis of Distributed Systems, Formal Verification, and Next-Generation Compute Architectures
latest.description: This analysis examines AWS infrastructure innovations: the Nitro System's secret-free hypervisors, S3's formally verified strong consistency, DynamoDB's distributed transactions, and Lambda SnapStart's 91% cold start reduction. AWS employs lightweight formal methods (TLA+/P), custom Graviton silicon with 63% better price-performance, and chaos engineering for resilience. These systems support millions of workloads across 33 regions through hardware-software co-design and continuous verification.

latest.title: From IAM to EC2: Building a Secure AWS Mini-Architecture with Billing Control and Access Governance
latest.description:
Learning AWS becomes meaningful when you stop treating services as isolated tools and start seeing them as layers of a system: identity, compute, network, and cost governance working together.
This hands-on exercise builds a minimal but realistic AWS environment that reflects how cloud infrastructure is structured in production systems — with controlled access, monitored spending, and a deployed service running on EC2.

latest.title: From Spend Blindness to Cost Accountability: An Architect's Guide to Governing Amazon Bedrock at Scale
latest.description: A practical framework for platform engineers, cloud financial management teams, and AI governance leaders who need to turn Bedrock consumption into something the business can see, explain, and own.

latest.title:
My First AWS Hands-On Project — EC2, IAM, Security & Billing
AWS Cloud Club University of Ilorin Bootcamp 2026 | Week 3 | Team 11 — Trusted Roles
latest.description:
When they handed us the assignment document, my first reaction was honest — where do I even start?
The tasks looked like a lot. EC2 instances, IAM users, groups, roles, policies, billing alerts, deploying a web page, validating permissions. All in one assignment.
But I made a decision. Stop staring at it. Start doing it.
And that changed everything.

latest.title: RDS vs DynamoDB: How to Think About Data in the Cloud
latest.description: RDS and DynamoDB solve different problems. Learn the mental model for choosing between them based on your data and access patterns.

latest.title: AWS Nitro and CPU Graviton Meets Unikernels
latest.description: Rethink the cloud with Nanos Unikernel...

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Anukriti – What If Drug Trials Included Everyone?
trending.description: Simulating drug safety across diverse genomes to detect population-specific risks before clinical trials begin.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
trending.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

trending.title: Construye con Strands Agents en Nerdearla Chile 2026
trending.description: Crea tu primer proyecto con Strands Agents en Nerdearla del 17 y 18 de Abril 2026 en Centro GAM, Santiago, Chile.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Perspective
trending.description: Perspective is an AI-powered mental health companion that extracts clinical insight from free-form speech — no mood scales required. Using 4-cluster parallel extraction on Amazon Bedrock, validated against the Subjective Units of Distress Scale (SUDS), Work and Social Adjustment Scale (WSAS), PHQ-9, and GAD-7, it serves undiagnosed users and diagnosed patients alike. Clinician-validated by three practising doctors. Built on AWS serverless with Kiro.

trending.title: Cómo crear un agente de IA desde cero. Open source, local y gratis.
trending.description: De un modelo que solo responde a uno que busca, razona y recuerda. Todo con código que puedes leer, forkear y contribuir.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: RetainIQ
trending.description: Detect knowledge concentration risk before it costs you. RetainIQ scores every critical topic 0–100 and triggers AI interventions. Zero docs effort required.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: CareLens AI — Transparent Care Guidance When It Matters Most.
trending.description: CareLens is a privacy-first AI platform that helps people understand healthcare costs before seeking treatment. By analyzing de-identified symptoms, location, and optional insurance details, it compares care options like ER, urgent care, or specialists and surfaces real-world price ranges using hospital transparency and CMS data. It doesn’t diagnose—it delivers clear, grounded cost, bill analyzing and care guidance so users can make confident decisions without surprise bills.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Veloquity - The Agentic Evidence Intelligent Platform Turning Raw Feedback into Evidence-Driven Decisions
trending.description: Decisions don’t fail from too little data - they fail from too much noise. Veloquity, an agentic evidence intelligence platform, filters that noise by transforming fragmented feedback into validated evidence and delivering explainable, prioritized recommendations with full source-to-decision traceability that guides human decisions. READ ON TO SEE HOW.
